opps.title: Application Development and Support Services - FMCS
opps.description:
The General Services Administration, Office of Centralized Acquisition Services, on behalf of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service, is issuing this Request for Quote to establish a single-award Blanket Purchase Agreement under GSA Multiple Award Schedule SIN 54151S Information Technology Professional Services.

FMCS requires Application Development and Support Services to maintain, support, modernize, secure, integrate, and enhance its enterprise application environment. The FMCS environment includes Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform, Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI, Dataverse, LMS365, Azure Government / GCC Medium, custom .NET/C# applications, SQL Server, PostgreSQL where applicable, Collabspace Enterprise Records Management, CCMS, Arbitration systems, the FMCS public web environment, AWS-hosted workloads, and legacy systems.

The scope includes project management, operations and maintenance, minor enhancements, major enhancements, application development, modernization and migration support, cloud migration support, cybersecurity compliance support, ATO/POA&M support, Section 508/accessibility support, records management support, documentation, reporting, quality control, transition support, and related technical services.

The BPA will include the following service CLINs:

CLIN 0001 Project Management
CLIN 0002 Operations and Maintenance and Minor Enhancements
CLIN 0003 Major Enhancements, Modernization, and Migration
The Government anticipates issuing Initial BPA Call 0001 concurrently with BPA award for Base Period ADSS Services. The anticipated BPA period of performance consists of one 12-month base period and three 12-month option periods. The estimated BPA ceiling is $4,500,000. The BPA does not obligate funds; funds will be obligated only through BPA Calls issued by the Contracting Officer.

This RFQ is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside under GSA MAS SIN 54151S. Only quotes submitted by GSA Sch

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 25-50 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@st

opps.title: Integrating Technology into the Delivery of Clinical Care at Health Centers
opps.description:
The purpose of this initiative is to advance the integration of technology, digital innovation (through AI and patient wearable devices) to support more efficient chronic disease prevention and control, with a focus on hypertension, diabetes, and obesity, across HRSA-funded health centers and to reduce health center administrative burden. 
Wearable devices, including smartwatches, rings, fitness trackers, and continuous glucose monitors (CGMs), generate continuous patient health data that, when securely integrated into electronic health record (EHR) systems, equip providers with timely, actionable insights to improve clinical decision-making, close gaps in care, and support value-based reimbursement. 
This contract will also incorporate sister agency initiatives, where applicable, including the Center for Medicare & Medicaid Service's (CMS's) Advancing Chronic Care with Effective Scalable Solutions (ACCESS), Food and Drug Administration's (FDA's) Technology-Enabled Meaningful Patient Outcomes (TEMPO) for Digital Health Devices Pilot, and the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology (ONC) interoperability standards, including the United States Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) and USCDI+ guidance to complement the five pillars of the HHS AI Strategy.
By improving digital and health literacy for both patients and providers, this initiative centers on a patient-focused approach; empowering patients, strengthening shared decision-making, and aligning with HHS's Making America Healthy Again (MAHA) initiative. Through tailored technical assistance, peer learning, and evaluation, this program will help health centers implement strategies that measurably improve outcomes for patients and communities across the country.

opps.title: FTCA Compliant Risk Management Certification Program
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to enable HRSA to fulfill its statutory obligations under 42 U.S.C. 233 by strengthening patient safety, risk management, and operational consistency across FTCA-deemed health centers through a standardized, nationally recognized training and certification program leveraging an existing solution tailored to HRSA needs. This effort will establish clear and enforceable competency benchmarks for risk management professionals, ensuring alignment with FTCA requirements and federal expectations for oversight, accountability, and risk mitigation.
The program will promote consistency in risk management practices, including documentation, credentialing oversight, and claims analysis, improving the defensibility of clinical care and reducing avoidable malpractice exposure to the Federal Government. By equipping staff with the necessary knowledge and skills, it will enhance compliance, strengthen risk identification and mitigation, and support a culture of patient safety, ultimately standardizing risk management capabilities and reducing variability across the Health Center Program.
To establish and deliver a comprehensive, standardized healthcare risk management training and certification solution that leverages an existing, nationally recognized certification program, tailored to meet HRSA and FTCA requirements, and equips health center and free clinic staff in ambulatory care settings with the knowledge and proficiency to effectively manage risk. This initiative supports HRSA's statutory responsibilities under 42 U.S.C. 233 by building core competencies to enable participants to lead, implement, and sustain risk management programs; improving consistency in documentation, credentialing oversight, and risk analysis; and strengthening compliance with FTCA requirements. The program will address inconsistencies in current practices, enhance patient safety, and reduce federal malpractice exposure by improving the defensibility of care. It will

opps.title: Avaya PBX Phone System Replacement for Glacier National Park
opps.description:
Glacier National Park (GLAC) currently operates two legacy Avaya/Nortel CS1000 Private Branch Exchange (PBX) systems interconnected via T1 trunks between the Park Headquarters in West Glacier and the Saint Mary Visitor Center complex. The existing infrastructure supports approximately 300 IP/VoIP endpoints (Avaya 1120E handsets), 150 analog lines (serving fax, alarm, and OPX circuits extending up to 24 miles), and voicemail services for approximately 400 mailboxes.

The current CS1000 platform has reached end-of-life (EOL) and end-of-support (EOS) status, so the purpose of this requirement is to procure and install an Avaya IP Office platform at the Headquarters and Saint Mary locations, including 300 Avaya J179 IP phone handsets, software licensing, auto attendant/phone tree and voicemail configuration, 3-year maintenance support, and professional installation services.

This IS a brand name requirement.

This IS a total small business set-aside.

Contract Type: The contract awarded as a result of this RFQ will be a firm-fixed price commercial contract.

Questions are due at least 5 BUSINESS DAYS before the quote due date, making them due by COB 7/21/2026 at the latest.

NOTE: Your company must also be registered in System for Award Management (SAM) at the time of award and throughout the entire award/contract performance - The registration must be "active" in SAM.gov. - Government will award to the technically acceptable offer from a responsible offeror, that represents the best value to the government

*** BE ADVISED - THE PRICE IN GSA eBUY WILL BE THE EVALUATED PRICE ***
CONTRACTING POC: Kiara Stewart - kiara_stewart@nps.gov

opps.title: Treatment for Breast Cancer
opps.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
opps.description:
The work to be performed under this contract includes: a) Project management and communication; b) Development of methodology, including criteria that will be used to select study sites and study participants, and approaches that will be used to collect the required data; c) Development and testing of survey and other data collection instruments; d) Preparation of IRB/OMB packages; e) Implementation of survey and other instruments to collect the required data; f) Data analysis and reporting of findings; and g) Preparation of study summary report. The Contractor shall work with CDC and the study sites to successfully achieve the objectives of this project. The Contractor shall be responsible for documenting progress during the contract. CDC will provide input on all aspects of the work to be performed by the Contractor.
The Contractor shall provide all labor, equipment, materials, supplies, and travel required to complete all tasks of the contract. Contractor performance and resulting deliverables must adhere to all federal, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, and/or CDC IT security policies and procedures.

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.

The D&PE API Services organization seeks contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain critical middleware applications and shared services

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.title: Financial Management and Business Support Services
opps.description:
Sources Sought Notice - #2032H326N00011
Financial Management and Business Support Services

1.0	SYNOPSIS
The Treasury Common Services Center (TCSC) provides shared acquisition and operational support services across the Department of the Treasury (Treasury). As part of this mission, TCSC Acquisition Services supports Treasury-wide procurement reporting, analytics, data quality, and acquisition oversight activities.

The U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of the Deputy Chief Financial Officer (ODCFO) requires specialized financial analysis, accounting, and business services to support its business operations.  These services will help improve operational efficiencies, systems and data integrity, and support the implementation of new and current agency-wide business initiatives. Responses to this Sources Sought Notice (SSN) will help ODCFO assess the marketplace for contractors capable of delivering the required services.

The contractor shall provide financial management and accounting support services to include technical research of GAAP, OMB, and legislative requirements, accounting and analytical services in areas such as proprietary and budgetary reporting, variance analysis, problem resolution, data reconciliation, abnormal accounting conditions and business process improvements while also helping to maintain robust internal controls that facilitate Treasury's compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and guidance.  The Contractor must provide all management, labor, and supervision necessary to satisfy Performance Work Statement (PWS) requirements.

The Treasury has not determined the acquisition approach. Any results of this SSN will assist the Treasury Departmental Offices and the Departmental Offices & Enterprise Procurement Branch, TCSC Acquisition Services in determining an acquisition strategy about how to proceed.

This is NOT a formal solicitation or request for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotes. The purpose of this notice is to obtain i

opps.title: Sources Sought-FTCA-Compliant Risk Management Certification Program
opps.description:
The purpose of this requirement is to enable HRSA to fulfill its statutory obligations under 42 U.S.C. 233 by strengthening patient safety, risk management, and operational consistency across FTCA-deemed health centers through a standardized, nationally recognized training and certification program leveraging an existing solution tailored to HRSA needs. This effort will establish clear and enforceable competency benchmarks for risk management professionals, ensuring alignment with FTCA requirements and federal expectations for oversight, accountability, and risk mitigation.
The program will promote consistency in risk management practices, including documentation, credentialing oversight, and claims analysis, improving the defensibility of clinical care and reducing avoidable malpractice exposure to the Federal Government. By equipping staff with the necessary knowledge and skills, it will enhance compliance, strengthen risk identification and mitigation, and support a culture of patient safety, ultimately standardizing risk management capabilities and reducing variability across the Health Center Program.

opps.title: Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC)
opps.agency: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
opps.description: The Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) Medical Record Review Contractor (MRRC) program is a CMS initiative designed to safeguard the integrity and accuracy of payments made to Medicare Advantage (MA) plans. As the primary mechanism through which CMS validates payment accuracy, the RADV program relies on this contractor to perform and support a comprehensive suite of medical record review activities. These activities include the intake of medical records, abstraction of diagnosis codes, quality assurance and continuous improvement efforts, appeals support, and systems and reporting enhancements all of which work together to ensure that MA plan payments are based on accurate and verified clinical documentation. This task order is issued under the Center for Program Integrity (CPI) Payment Accuracy Acquisition Strategy, reflecting CMS's broader commitment to program integrity and responsible stewardship of federal healthcare funds.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
After the completion of the State Laws digest on illegal passing, a more in-depth review or state of knowledge report is needed to determine if a model law for illegal passing would help with the consistency of enforcement of these driving violations. More clarity if needed on how currently laws are enforced to help better inform states to improve enforcement of these laws. The state laws digest informed that laws vary in content, organization, consistency, and clarity. Also, that what may constitute illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neigh-boring one. States also vary in their methods to review, document and report violations including permissibility of stop-arm cameras. This report is needed to give clarity on how states can best improve enforcement of this law.

To address this need, this project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This study will provide if a model law would be necessary for continuity among enforcement within the U.S States and provide best example of a model law.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is seeking to enhance the enforcement of laws against the illegal passing of school buses, a significant safety concern for students. 

The project aims to address inconsistencies in enforcement and prosecution across various jurisdictions, as current laws vary widely in their content and clarity. A previous digest indicated that illegal passing violations are often dismissed in court, highlighting the need for improved education and advocacy among judicial officials. This solicitation seeks to conduct a comprehensive revie

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open Solicitation
opps.description:
July 7, 2026: This is Amendment 0009 to solicitation 47QRCA23R0002.

The General Services Administration (GSA), Federal Acquisition Service (FAS), Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD), is issuing Amendment 0009 in accordance with RFO-2025-05 Supplement 1 FAR 5.101 for the One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+), Indefinite-Delivery, Indefinite-Quantity (IDIQ), Multi-Agency Contracts (MAC). The System for Award Management (SAM.gov) is the official source for information about this procurement.

The following documents and attachments are updated as a result of Amendment 0009:


	OASIS+ 8(a) RFP (current as of Amendment 0009)
	SF30 (Amendment 0009) and Continuation Sheet


Provisional Attachments


	J.P-1 OASIS+ Domain Qualifications Matrix and Scorecards
	J.P-2 FPDS Sample
	J.P-3 Project Verification Form
	J.P-4 Domains Auto-Relevant NAICS Codes and PSCs
	J.P-9 Cost/Price Template
	J.P-14 Lateral Springboarding Summary Sheet


Contract Attachments


	J-2 Task Order, Transactional Data, and Contract Access Fee Reporting
	J-4 Department of Defense Required Provisions and Clauses for Task Orders
	J-5 Task Order Clause and Provision Matrix


Consistent with Section H.11 of the OASIS+ RFP, GSA intends to maintain continuously open solicitations to support rolling admissions/on-ramping and to allow existing awardees, when eligible, to compete for additional Domains as Government needs evolve. Should GSA determine a need to close any domain or solicitation, the agency will provide public notification. Offerors are permitted to submit a single proposal for multiple domains under a single solicitation. There is no fixed timeline for award; timelines will vary based on the volume of proposals received and are subject to resource availability.

Offerors must be registered and active in SAM.gov. All proposals must be submitted through the OASIS+ SubmissionPortal (OSP). The OSP is open for registration and submission of offers as of January 12,

opps.title: eAMLIS Operations and Maintenance and CYBERSECURITY
opps.agency: Office of the Secretary of the Interior
opps.description:
The U.S. Department of the Interior, Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement (OSMRE), seeks quotations from GSA MAS contractors for continued Operations & Maintenance (O&M), ETL/GIS workflow support, system enhancements, legislative/policy update integration, cybersecurity compliance, and transition planning for the Enhanced Abandoned Mine Land Inventory System (eAMLIS).
eAMLIS is a mature, mission-critical system supporting statutory obligations under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act (SMCRA). It provides annually reported AML inventory information to DOI leadership, Congress, States, Tribes, and external stakeholders. System requirements, technical objectives, and operational responsibilities are detailed in the attached Statement of Objectives (SOO).

The Contractor shall provide all personnel, tools, equipment, and services necessary to maintain stable system operations, support ETL pipelines and geospatial features, execute modernization efforts, and ensure timely updates in response to legislative changes and federal audits (including OIG recommendations).

A hybrid Firm-Fixed-Price (FFP) and Labor-Hour (LH) order is anticipated.

The Government intends to award a task order from this solicitation to the responsible offeror whose offer conforming to the solicitation will be most advantageous to the Government, price and other factors considered. The following factors shall be used to evaluate offers: 
Factor 1: Technical Approach (Most Important)
Factor 2: Relevant Experience & Past Performance
Factor 3: Price

The Government will select the best-suited vendor using a comparative evaluation process IAW FAR 80405-2(c)(3). 

Please see attached solicitation (B06 - SOL-eAMLIS) for full RFQ details.

opps.summary:
Background
 The Embassy of the United States of America in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, is seeking a content creation partner to produce video content aimed at engaging online audiences on U.S. policy priorities. 

The goal of this contract is to create high-quality videos that can effectively communicate key messages and enhance the embassy's digital presence.

Work Details
 The contractor shall create twelve (12) high-fidelity, cinematic videos, each between 30-60 seconds long, optimized for social media distribution across platforms such as Instagram Reels, Facebook, X, LinkedIn, and YouTube.

opps.title: DHS CUBE
opps.description:
REQUEST FOR INFORMATION. THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY.  
This RFI is for planning purposes only and shall not be construed as an obligation on the part of the Government.  This is NOT a Request for Quotations or Proposals.  No solicitation document exists, and a formal solicitation may or may not be issued by the Government as a result of the responses received to this RFI.  The Government will not pay for any response or demonstration expenses.  Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary or competition sensitive information contained in their response.  Responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract.  Those who respond to this RFI should not anticipate feedback concerning their submission.  All submissions will become the property of the Government and will not be returned.  Responding to the RFI is completely voluntary and will not enhance or adversely affect responses to any resulting solicitation if such solicitation is issued at a later date.  There is no competitive advantage gained by any of the respondents to this RFI.

BACKGROUND

The DHS Office of the Chief Readiness Support Officer (OCRSO) requires contract support for the DHS Cube, which is an information technology tool that integrates the Department's financial, acquisition, human capital, contracting, assets, and security data to support analysis and decision making. Through the DHS Cube, the Department is creating an integrated structure that allows data to be utilized more efficiently in scenarios ranging from day-to-day activities to incident management decision-making by the Department's senior-most officials.
The objective is to provide information technology (IT), software development, and business functional support associated with the operations, maintenance, enhancement, maturation, and data population of the DHS Cube system and capabilities. The DHS Cube is a Microsoft Azure data warehouse

opps.title: International Procurement Experts
opps.description:
The Millennium Challenge Corporation (MCC), Program Acquisition & Assistance Practice Group (PAA) within the Department of Compact Operations (DCO) seeks to award contract with a firm to provide multiple procurement experts on an as-needed basis with extensive international procurement experience under donor funded projects, such as funded by the World Bank, MCC, Asian Development Bank etc. These experts will support MCC PAA in developing procurement policy, guidance, and tools for use by MCC partner country Accountable Entities (MCAs) in their procurement operations and assist MCC PAA in measuring performance of those operations and recommending improvements in procedures and application of tools. The Contractor will assist MCC PAA in guiding the MCAs and their procurement agents through providing training, advice, hands-on support on MCC's procurement policies, procedures, guidance and standard bidding documents, procurement-related technologies, market outreach efforts, and helping them in overseeing the compliance thereto to meet MCC requirements. Extensive interaction with MCC will be required for the experts to fully understand MCC's procurement documents and/or to advise MCC in modifying these documents as and when needed.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

opps.description:
Non-personal services to provide network sustainment and deployment support services to the Military Health System (MHS) Medical Treatment Facilities (MTFs) both inside and outside the continental United States (CONUS and OCONUS). This work includes functions such as network performance
measurement and monitoring, assistance with network design and development, network measurement, customer service in the form of trouble tracking and troubleshooting at the Tier 0 level, onsite support for both contractor and Government support teams for escalated trouble tickets, as well as maintenance and sparing support. Such services will be provided in accordance with stated MHS and specific Medical Component priorities. Services shall also comply with existing and evolving technical architecture guidance from international, commercial, DoD, and Health Affairs (HA) sources.  These services will be provided in a centralized and decentralized mode. The on-site network specialists will be the primary 24/7 on-call decentralized points of contact within the MTFs. Additional support and guidance will be provided by regional network engineers and DHA centralized infrastructure team.

Background
 The National Tobacco Education Campaign is initiated by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to combat tobacco use, which is the leading cause of preventable disease and death in the United States. 

The campaign aims to educate adults aged 18 and older about the harmful effects of tobacco use and secondhand smoke exposure, as well as the benefits of quitting. The CDC's National Tobacco Control Program (NTCP) has been operational since 1999, providing funding and support to state and territorial tobacco control programs to reduce tobacco-related diseases and deaths.

THIS IS A SOURCES SOUGHT ANNOUNCEMENT ONLY.  This notice does not constitute a commitment by the Government.  All information submitted in response to this announcement is voluntary, and the Government will not pay for information requested nor will it compensate any respondent for any cost incurred in developing information provided to the Government.  

This Sources Sought Notice is to identifying potential sources and determine if there exists an adequate number of qualified interested contractors capable of providing solutions to meet the requirement.  The Government may use the responses to this Sources Sought for information and planning purposes.  
This notice is seeking a Small Business Certified Woman Owned Business to provide  staffing augmentation in support of risk management, financial analysis, special project management and support, and other key functions supporting the US Small Business Administration, Office of Credit Risk Management.  

This Sources Sought is issued for information and planning purposes only and does not constitute an RFQ/RFP.  No Request For Quote (RFQ) / Request for Proposal (RFP) exists; therefore, do not request a copy of the RFQ/RFP.  The Government does not intend to award a contract based only on responses to this Sources Sought.

All information received in response to this Sources Sought marked "Proprietary" will be protected and handled accordingly. Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary or competition sensitive information contained in their response. The Government reserves the right to ask follow-up clarifying questions to individual companies based on proprietary information submitted without posting those questions.
Responses to the Sources Sought will not be returned.  Responders are solely responsible for all expenses associated with responding to this Sources Sought. 

The Government requests that interested parties respond to this notice in writing including details of your company

This is an RFI for a non-personal services contract requirement, under which the Contractor is an independent Contractor to provide Employment Coaches to support the Employment Support Program for the Florida National Guard J9. opps.title: STRATEGIC INDUSTRIAL BASE SERVICES (SIBS)
opps.description:
This is a Request for Information (RFI) for market research purposes only. This is not a solicitation for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotations. The purpose of this RFI is to identify sources with the capability to provide the highly specialized strategic industrial base services detailed in the attached draft Performance Work Statement (PWS).

The Office of the Assistant Secretary of the Air Force for Acquisition, Technology and Logistics (SAF/AQ) is seeking information on contractors who can provide support services including, but not limited to, defense industrial base intelligence, continuous financial forensics, Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE) analysis, and enterprise negotiation strategy. The objective is to integrate the intersection of Government Contracting (GovCon) law and corporate financial drivers to mitigate program risk and optimize acquisition planning for the Department of the Air Force (DAF).

The Government will not award a contract on the basis of this RFI, nor will it reimburse any costs associated with preparing a response. Responses to this RFI are voluntary and will be used to determine the appropriate acquisition strategy for this requirement.

Please carefully review the attached RFI and PWS before providing a response.

opps.title: Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC)
opps.description: Air Forice Office of Special Investigation (AFOSI) has a requirement for  The Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC) integrates and audits criminal history records from law enforcement and judge advocate professionals to deliver accurate and timely criminal history records to external stakeholders ensuring public safety and the rights of the individual.  The organization is aligned under the commander of the DAF Office of Special Investigations (OSI) and serves as the primary focal point for the exchange of criminal justice information between DAF entities and US and foreign government criminal justice jurisdictions.  This contract requirement increases capacity of a training team, an audit team, an operations team, and a mission support team.  The Contractor shall interface and perform technical and programmatic liaison support across the DAF-CJIC and with DAF Headquarters elements conducting or interfacing with indexing or criminal justice information management requirements, operations, and planning.  The Contractor shall provide support to perform information and records management utilizing multiple government databases to request, receive, inventory, and prepare information for entry into criminal justice databases internal and external to the DAF.  Additionally, the Contractor shall support related administrative functions in support of personnel assigned to the DAF-CJIC, various DAF entities that accomplish indexing operations, and/or indexing task forces IAW the PWS.

opps.title: Facility Inspection & Detainee Death Review Services
opps.description:
The Office of Professional Responsibility (OPR) is an organizational element within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The Office of Detention Oversight (ODO) is a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office (ISP). ODO conducts compliance inspections of detention facilities in which detainees are housed for more than seventy-two (72) hours to determine compliance with the ICE 2025 and 2019 National Detention Standards (NDS), the 2025 Non-Dedicated Intergovernmental Detention Standards (NDIDS), the 2011 Performance Based National Detention Standards (PBNDS), as applicable, and the 2020 Family Residential Standards (FRS).
The External Reviews and Analysis Unit (ERAU) is also a unit within OPR's Inspections Program Office. ERAU is responsible for conducting administrative reviews of all deaths of individuals in ICE custody. The Detainee Death Review (DDR) is a focused, fact finding, objective review of the facts and circumstances surrounding a detainee's death. ERAU also conducts special and focused reviews, as requested by ICE leadership.
The Office of Acquisition Management (OAQ), on behalf of ICE/ERAU is seeking a vendor who shall provide support services related to inspections and detainee death reviews to determine facility compliance with the NDS, NDIDS, PBNDS, or FRS, and for supporting designated analytical activities within ODO and ERAU.  The vendor will provide expert operational, analytical and administrative support for ODO and ERAU inspections, reviews, and special assignments.
The vendor shall provide productivity and management support, including but not limited to, quality control; data input; clerical support; statistical and analytical documentation and data manipulation; training of personnel; and other related functions. The vendor's project managers for both inspections and DDRs shall perform day to day management of overall contract support operations and ensure conformity with all contract

These inspections assess compliance with various detention standards, including the ICE National Detention Standards (NDS) and Family Residential Standards (FRS).

The contractor will be required to provide all personnel and expertise required to perform comprehensive administrative, personnel, and physical security support to the 89th Attack Squadron (89 ATKS) at Ellsworth Air Force Base, South Dakota. opps.title: 165 ASOS HPO Services
opps.description:
Special Warfare (SW)/Tactical Air Control Party (TACP) in the Air National Guard (ANG) must always be ready to perform at their peak in order to accomplish their challenging mission set.  Attaining and maintaining this level of peak human performance requires the implementation of a top-notch physical conditioning and maintenance program comparable to those seen in Olympic-level and professional sports organizations.  The Human Performance Optimization (HPO) program will focus on the whole operator concept to not only assist TACP operators in accomplishing their military missions in the short term, but also to emphasize the maintenance of peak performance over the long term in order to lengthen the service life of the "human weapon system" while ensuring high-quality production.  The contractor shall provide all personnel, equipment, tools, materials, supervision, and quality control necessary, except as specified in Paragraph 3.0 as Government Furnished, to perform Preservation of the Force and Family Services, as defined in this PWS.

A Site Visit will not be conducted for this acquisition.

Any and all questions are required to be submitted No Later Than close of business 21 July 2026.  Answers will be provided no later than close of business 28 July 2026.

Please submit all questions to:  justin.smith.173@us.af.mil and andrew.headrick@us.af.mil.

opps.title: This procurement is for access to the comprehensive law library via UHD Hard Disk with a site license for unlimited use in support of Naval Consolidated Brig Miramar.
opps.agency: NAVSUP Fleet Logistics Center San Diego
opps.description:
Notice of Intent to Sole Source

THIS IS A NOTICE OF INTENT TO AWARD A SOLE SOURCE CONTRACT AND IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE QUOTES. Reference: Solicitation Number N00244-22-Q-0245. Naval Supply Systems Command (NAVSUP) Fleet Logistics Center San Diego (FLCSD) intends to award a firm fixed price contract under the authority of FAR 6.302-1(a)(2)(i)(A), only one source being available to meet the Government’s needs, to:

 RELX, Inc. - DBA: LexisNexis
 9443 Springboro Pike 
 Miamisburg, OH 45342-4425  

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this requirement is 511199; the business size standard is 500 (number of employees).

The objective of this sole source award is to issue a Firm Fixed-Price (FFP) contract for LexisNexis UHD, consisting of a multitude of legal resources, in support of the Naval Consolidated Brig Miramar (NAVCONBRIG Miramar).
SECNAVISNT 1640.9C and American Correctional Association standards requires our command to provide comprehensive resources for legal research on Military and Federal Law. Military Correctional Facility Miramar does not allow prisoners internet access so this is the only and or least expensive option. The UHD (Hard Disk) allows prisoners to search a multitude of legal databases on their time and without the requirement of staff supervision. If this system were not used, it would require many man hours to supervise each prisoner requiring access to legal materials. Additionally, the prisoners would not have ready use of legal materials, which may preclude them from meeting certain deadlines for clemency or trial.
The UHD is provided by Lexis Nexis with a site license for unlimited use by prisoners.

RELX, Inc. DBA: LexisNexis is the standard all Legal offices use and is the only electronic law library service available on a hard disk drive because prisoners cannot have internet. Only RELX was identified that offered this solution. The only other law library services available are onli

opps.title: AF12--Intent to Sole Source    Scientific Journal Supplement Publication Service -  Academic Emergency Medicine
opps.description:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, VA Boston Healthcare System, Center for Information Dissemination and Education Resources (CIDER) intends to negotiate on a sole source basis, pursuant to FAR 6.302-1, with the Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM) for scientific journal supplement publication services. 

CIDER requires services to produce a special journal supplement of peer-reviewed manuscripts in the Academic Emergency Medicine (AEM) Journal of Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM). The manuscripts written by VA Health Service Research and Development Service (HSR&D) and invited investigators/respondents will be focused on the  state of the art of emergency care in the VA healthcare system.  AEM/SAEM is uniquely qualified due to its emphasis on clinical trials, observational cohort studies, and economic or policy research, all of which are a part of health services research; its ranking as a top journal in the field; and its target audience and membership cohort of academic emergency physicians to produce a special journal supplement.
 
This notice of intent is not a request for competitive proposals; however, any responsible source who believes it is capable of meeting the requirement may submit a capability statement to the contracting office no later than Wednesday, February 23, 2022, 3:00 PM, EST.  Interest/capability statements may be sent to Shannon Hukriede at Shannon.Hukriede@va.gov. No telephone responses will be accepted. A determination not to compete the proposed requirement based upon the responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

opps.title: Master Synch
opps.description:
The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA.

The Video Lab at the NAVCC is tasked with the digitization of multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video. It was designed and built to substantially emulate a video production facility and uses an Evertz Master Sync Generator system. This system provides crucial reference video test signals for the video lab. It allows the VTR’s (video tape recorder) to lock on to a reference signal which provides the most optimal conditions for error free digitization of analog video and audio into the digital signal which is then used by the encoders to create archival files. The current system is obsolete and has been damaged beyond repair in certain areas such as the GPS antenna and cabling, which has suffered rodent damage.

The Library needs a contractor that will provide system hardware, installation and documentation for the replacement Master Sync system in the Video Lab at NAVCC.
opps.summary:

The Video Lab at NAVCC is tasked with digitizing multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video using an Evertz Master Sync Generator system, which is currently obsolete and damaged beyond repair.

opps.summary:
Background

opps.title: Sources Sought - DCMA Blue List Program Support Services
opps.description:
This is a sources sought posting to confirm the capabilities of GSA OASIS contractors to meet our Government requirement. NOTICE: This is NOT a solicitation, and no award will be made as a result of this Sources Sought Posting. 

The Government requirement is to hire 11 Full-Time Employees (FTE) in the following areas: One (1) Task Lead, two (2) Project Managers, two (2) IT Project Managers, two (2) IT Cybersecurity Specialists, one (1) Management Analyst, two (2) Contract Administrators, and one (1) Public Affairs/Media Outreach Specialist for one base year and one option year. This effort supports the agency's bona fide need to secure the sUAS supply chain, accelerate procurement timelines, and eliminate administrative barriers for warfighters seeking to acquire secure drone technology.

Attached is a DRAFT Performance Work Statement for industry review, and the Government is looking for feedback and any information that can help with creating an official solicitation. We are anticipating to release the official Request for Proposal in August, with award in September. Please provide any questions, feedback, or any insights possible to help make this possible. 

- How feasible are the requirements laid out in the Performance Work Statement (PWS) attached?
- How quickly, upon hiring, could you hire, onboard, and relocate (as applicable) to meet FTE needs?
- Does the information provided in the PWS give clear direction to the tasks being requested?
- Are there any recommended items or details that should be added in the PWS that may be unclear?

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.title: Depot Operations Support
opps.description:
The United States Department of Agriculture's (USDA) Client Experience Center (CEC) is accountable for managing the Information Technology (IT) infrastructure and service delivery to USDA. 

The USDA Enterprise Depot's primary mission is to provide centralized and standardized device deployment and excessing services to over 100,000 USDA customers across the United States and its territories. 

The USDA Enterprise Depot is a hybrid facility that performs receiving, storage, inventory management, asset management, device (laptop, desktop, and tablet) preparation, OS imaging/deployment, kitting, pick and ship, returns processing, shipping, and excessing of IT equipment such as laptops, desktops, tablets, mobile printers, monitors, cords, keyboards, flash drives, cable locks, and mice. The USDA Enterprise Depot is partnered and integrated with 3rd party vendors to assist with overflow storage and shipping services.

opps.description:
Provide services in accordance with the attached Statement of Work. The primary goal of establishing this contract is for electronic security consulting services to support the procurement and design phases of the U.S. Marshals Service (USMS) electronic security system project for Courthouses in Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN.

Please read the attached documents for information on the scope of work and additional information included. The consulting services outlined focus on supporting GSA through scope development, design review, procurement, and initial security contractor onboarding for new electronic security systems (Access Control, Video Surveillance, Intrusion Detection, and Intercom/Emergency Communication). Services will primarily be performed remotely, with necessary on-site activities at Washington, D.C., Atlanta, GA, Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN. The estimated duration for these services is 2-years from the commencement date, with implementation support projected from August 3, 2026, to August 3, 2028.

The Government intends to award an order for services in accordance with FAR 8.4 (GSA Class Deviation RFO-2025-08) on the basis of best value to the Government. All evaluation factors other than cost or price, when combined, are approximately equal to price. 

The technical factors that will be evaluated are the offerors Past Experience and Key Personnel, and will be of equal weight when evaluating the proposals.

Past Experience

The Offeror shall demonstrate past experience designing, installing, integrating, testing, and commissioning electronic security systems for facilities of similar size, scope, and complexity. Relevant systems include access control, video surveillance, intrusion detection, duress alarms, intercom systems, alarm monitoring, and related security infrastructure.

opps.title: Medical Records Reviews for NNMC
opps.description:
The Indian Health Service, Northern Navajo Medical Center (NNMC), located in Shiprock, New Mexico intends to negotiate a sole source award with Hardenbergh Group Inc. The North American Industry Classification System or NAICS code is 541618 Other Management Consulting Services $19M.



The required services include independent physician peer review, Initial Professional Practice Evaluations (IPPE), Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluations (OPPE), and professional practice reviews for inpatient and outpatient medical records. The contractor shall provide board-certified physician reviewers who are independent, free from conflicts of interest, and qualified in the applicable specialty under review. Reviews may be required in multiple clinical disciplines, including but not limited to Emergency Medicine, Pediatrics, Internal Medicine, Rheumatology, Family Medicine, General Surgery, Obstetrics and Gynecology, Orthopedics, Podiatry and Wound Care, Optometry, Neurology, Pathology, Radiology, Rehabilitation Services, and Psychiatry.



The contractor must be capable of performing timely medical record reviews, including urgent reviews with completion generally required within twenty-one (21) days of receipt of records. The contractor shall facilitate direct communication between hospital leadership and designated physician reviewers, provide reviewer qualifications and curriculum vitae upon request, and maintain established review procedures. All reviews must assess whether the provider's care and documentation meet the applicable specialty standard of care.



The contractor shall comply with all applicable privacy and confidentiality requirements, including HIPAA Business Associate obligations, maintain secure handling of protected health information, and ensure proper disposition of records following completion of reviews. The contractor shall also maintain the ability to provide additional review-related services as mutually agreed upon by the parties and assume r

opps.title: Ginnie Mae Financial Systems & Information Technology & Modernization Support
opps.description:
Ginnie Mae requires a Contractor to provide financial support services for its internal financial management and accounting operations. These services include support for the management of OCFO financial systems and the provision of secure access to those systems. The Contractor's overall support shall align with Ginnie Mae's mission and meet the functional requirements defined by the Federal Systems Integration Office and Ginnie Mae's Office of the Chief Financial Officer. Support shall advance modernization across the following areas: Program Management; Systems Operations and Maintenance; Systems Assessment and Modernization; Systems Enhancements; Project-Specific Enhancements and Assessments; Systems Infrastructure Collaboration; Application Provisioning; Security and IT Compliance; and Systems Training.

All quotes during Phase I must be submitted through the GSA eBuy web portal at: https://www.ebuy.gsa.gov. Quotes submitted by any other method may not be considered. This acquisition will use an advisory down-select, phased evaluation and source-selection process. The procurement will consist of a two-phase response and evaluation approach and in accordance with the RFQ.

Questions regarding Phase I of this solicitation must be submitted no later than July 20, 2026 at 10 a.m. EST. See RFQ Section 11.4.1 and 11.4.2 for additional details.

Phase I quotes must be submitted through the GSA eBuy web portal no later than July 28, 2026 at 10:00 a.m EST.

NOTE: The Government is accepting questions regarding Phase I only.

opps.title: R499--Service - Tele-Town Hall - Veterans Affairs Omaha VA
opps.description:
This is a Notice of Intent published in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 5.101(a)(1) requiring the dissemination of information regarding proposed contract actions. This Notice of Intent is for a proposed award of a sole source firm fixed price contract to the following contractor in support of Internet Town Hall Services in support of Nebraska Western-Iowa VA Health Care System, 4101 Woolworth Ave., Omaha, NE 68105-1850:

Broadnet
UEI: UDTNUZMDNFC8
2679 W. Main Street
Suite 757
Littleton, CO 80120

This is done under authority of 41 U.S.C. 3304(a)(1), as implemented by FAR 6.302-1: Only one source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements.

This is not a request for competitive quotes.

Market research revealed that Broadnet is the only internet townhall provider capable of providing the needed services for a quarterly online townhall to service the Veteran community within the Omaha, NE region (see attached Statement of Work for requirements).
 
The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this service is 512110 and the Product Service Code (PSC) is R499.

While this synopsis does not constitute a solicitation, interested vendors may identify their interest and capability to satisfy the requirement. 

All responsible sources may submit a capability statement, which shall be considered by the agency by e-mailing such statement and supporting documentation to Curt LaRose, Contracting Officer, curt.larose@va.gov by 0800 Central Time 07/20/2026. No phone calls. Please indicate  Response to Internet Townhall Services   NWI  in subject line of e-mail.


Such documentation will be reviewed solely for the purpose of determining whether or not to conduct the procurement on a competitive basis. A determination not to compete this requirement, based upon responses to this notice, is solely within the discretion of the Government under authorities cited above.

opps.title: Invasive Carp Regional Coordinating Committee -support services
opps.description:
This is a sources sought posting for the following support services:


Project Scope: 
The purpose of this project is to support ongoing efforts of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (FWS) and U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to convene the bi-national, 26-member Invasive Carp Regional Coordinating Committee (ICRCC) for the purpose of assisting ICRCC members in implementing their authorities to reduce and/or eliminate the threats posed by invasive carp to the Great Lakes. The USFWS and EPA serve as co-chairs of the ICRCC. This project includes support for development of the annual ICRCC Invasive Carp Action Plan. Support activities will be conducted at the direction of the EPA and FWS as the U.S. federal agency co-chairs of the ICRCC. The purpose of the ICRCC and the annual Action Plan is to assist ICRCC members in implementing their authorities to reduce and/or eliminate the threats posed by invasive carp to the Great Lakes. 

Project Description:
This project provides key staffing to support the effective operation of the ICRCC, under the leadership of the FWS and EPA as co-chairs. Due to the technical nature and time sensitiveness of tasks, experience with coordinating and managing invasive carp reporting and meetings in necessary. Efforts are to include but not limited to:

Ongoing activities to support operations and coordination of the ICRCC:
-	Meet virtually with the ICRCC co-chairs on regularly scheduled Teams calls twice weekly (or additional, as needed) to discuss the on-going, time-sensitive activities of the ICRCC. May include pre-meeting agenda development and keeping track of action items.
-	Support convening once-per-month (or as needed) ICRCC Teams platform video teleconferences for interagency discussion of relevant issues/updates on invasive carp management focused on supporting Great Lakes protection from invasive carp, and work with the co-chairs and member agencies to develop and distribute meeting materials and agendas. 
-	Support con

Price Proposal

The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) requires a Contractor to provide two (2) Journeyman Computer Support Specialists. opps.title: Providing Public Opinion Research Surveys.
opps.description:
Enclosed is a Request for Quotations (RFQ)for Public Opinion Research Surveys.

The U.S. Government intends to award a contract to the responsible company submitting an acceptable offer at the lowest price.  We intend to award a contract based on initial quotations, without holding discussions, although we may hold discussions with companies in the competitive range if there is a need to do so. We may also request clarification or best and final offers prior to award selection.

FOR QUESTIONS

Any and all questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing, in English, on or before the deadline. Questions must be sent to AmmanRFQQuestions@state.gov. The Subject Line of your email should read Question(s) re: 19J01026Q0014. The deadline is July 20, 2026.  The U.S. Embassy will publish all questions and answers as soon as possible after the deadline above.



HOW TO SUBMIT

To submit an offer or quotation, you must follow the instructions in Section 3 of the Solicitation; complete the required sections of the attached document(s); and submit a comprehensive offer package in a single email on or before the due date, according to the instructions below. Failure to follow any of the instructions will disqualify your proposal from being reviewed. Under no circumstances will late or incomplete packages be reviewed.


	
	
		Proposals/Offers are due on or before August 02, 2026 at 16:00. 
		All materials must be in English.
		Packages must be complete to be considered for award. Failure to complete or submit a required document or element will result in the offer being determined incomplete and will result in disqualification. You should carefully review the requirements in the full Solicitation as well as all supporting materials, to include this cover letter. Required materials typically include:
	
	





	SF-1449
	Section I, Pricing
	Section 5 Representations and Certifications
	Additional information as required in Section 3
	Proof of SAM Registration


NOT

opps.description:
Notice Type: Sources Sought
Notice ID: W50S6X26Q0008

Title: Vehicle Wrap Advertising - Delaware
NAICS Code: 541810 (Advertising Agencies) OR 541850 (Outdoor Advertising)

1. DISCLAIMER
This is a Sources Sought Notice only. This notice is issued solely for market research and planning purposes in accordance with FAR Part 10. It does NOT constitute a Request for Quote (RFQ), Request for Proposal (RFP), or a promise to issue a solicitation in the future. This notice does not commit the U.S. Government to contract for any supply or service whatsoever. The Government will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in response to this notice; all costs associated with responding to this notice will be solely at the interested party’s expense. Not responding to this notice does not preclude participation in any future competitive solicitation, if one is issued.

2. BACKGROUND & REQUIREMENT SUMMARY
The 166th Mission Support Contracting is conducting market research to identify potential small business and socioeconomic contractors capable of providing a targeted vehicle-wrap advertising and brand ambassador campaign. The contractor shall provide fully wrapped vehicles that will operate in highly visible, targeted zones across Delaware (Middletown, Dover, Georgetown/Milton, and Delaware Beach Towns).

The core deliverables include:


	Fleet Branding: Ten (10) fully wrapped vehicles – complete coverage (doors, hood, trunk, and back windows), to include installation and de-installation.
	Targeting & Vetting: Sourcing drivers whose daily routes align with the specified target zones. Drivers must pass comprehensive background checks.
	Collateral Distribution: Distribution of up to 2,000 Government-provided brochures (approx. 200 per vehicle) by the drivers.
	Analytics & Reporting: Monthly reporting to include Proof of Performance photos, GPS tracking heat maps, mileage tracking, and verified impression counts to calculate overall campai

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
As stated in the recently completed State Laws Digest for Illegal Passing of School Buses, the term "illegal passing" is a prevalent concept; however, the report illustrated its lack of universality. Variations in state laws dictate that passing a bus from the front on a divided highway may be illegal in some states but permissible under specific roadway conditions in others. What constitutes illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neighboring one. Given the high mobility of the U.S. population, the proximity of urban centers to multiple jurisdictions, and the significant disparities in the content and language of applicable Vehicle and Traffic Laws (VTL), confusion regarding the legality of actions surrounding a school bus is not unexpected.

The lack of clarity in drafting illegal passing provisions further compromises the ability of the typical motorist to understand these VTL requirements. Some jurisdictions have redrafted their amended provisions to include, among other things, the use of cameras. Thus, their updates present a mostly coherent, integrated statement of the law. Other jurisdictions have provisions that appear essentially sequential in design. New or changed provisions simply appear at the end of the existing VTL section, even if they logically relate to a topic much earlier in the material. This type of statutory construction is difficult to follow and can be misleading.

As recommended in the state laws digest, and in anticipation of future legislative interest, this project will focus more deeply on current illegal passing laws and develop a draft model state law to address incidents of illegal passing of stopped school buses. This report will also investigate the potential benefits and feasibility of the citation being deemed a moving violation.

This project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This project will provide if a model law would be n

 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is issuing this solicitation to address the growing concern for student safety on and around school buses. The project aims to develop a model law for the illegal passing of school buses, which varies significantly across jurisdictions, leading to confusion and inconsistent enforcement. The initiative is prompted by alarming statistics indicating that illegal passing incidents result in serious injuries and fatalities among children.

opps.title: Industry Day for Military Ocean Terminal Concord (MOTCO), CA
opps.description:
The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Sacramento District (SPK), invites industry partners to participate in an Industry Day focused on future projects at MOTCO. The Industry day will provide information on upcoming projects, seek technical information/solutions and seek input on current emerging acquisition strategies and project delivery methods. This event is intended to foster open dialogue between industry and USACE regarding technical solutions and innovative approaches to design and construction procurement.

BACKGROUND

The installation is composed of an approximately 4,197-acre (ac) Mission District and an approximately 115-ac Administrative District, which are connected by a stretch of Port Chicago Highway. The Mission District also includes 2,045-ac in offshore islands which are not part of the study area. The MOTCO installation lands were formerly Navy lands within Naval Weapons Station Seal Beach Detachment-Concord (NWSSBD-Concord). On October 1, 2008, the Mission and Administrative Districts properties were transferred from the Navy to the Army.  MOTCO is a power projection installation due to our strategic seaport supporting the Pacific Command (PACOM).  MOTCO provides strategic support through Ammo missions and delivers large combat readiness support to ensure lethality.

While the Government intends on providing project information on future acquisitions, the Government is also looking for technical solutions and direct feedback from industry to help shape future acquisition strategies and solicitation approaches.

EVENT OBJECTIVE

This Industry Day is designed to provide industry with a platform to:


	Gain insight on future projects and workload at MOTCO.
	Discuss successful implementation strategies, challenges, and risk mitigation approaches.
	Offer feedback on technical solutions to enhance construction efficiency.
	Provide recommendations on solicitation structure, evaluation criteria, partnering practices, and contract administration cons

All capability statements received by the due date of this notice will be considered by the Government.
